D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

22° Halo — Hexagonal Ice Prism

Cirrus-cloud hexagonal ice crystals act as 60° prisms; minimum deviation ≈ 22° produces the bright ring around sun or moon, with red on the inside edge

Ice n ≈ 1.31. For a 60° apex prism, minimum deviation occurs at symmetric passage: δmin = 2·arcsin(n·sin 30°) − 60° ≈ 21.84°. Outside that angle there is light, inside there is none — that's why the halo has a sharp inner edge and diffuse outer.

Slide ray's entry angle θ_i to see deviation curve and ring formation.
